calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-28-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Unopposed Motion For Approval Of Request To Dismiss Plaintiffs Paga Action Without Prejudice
Off Calendar Jun-03-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs'GISELE NORRIS AND HENRY YUAN unopposed motion for approval of request to dismiss plaintiffs' PAGA action without prejudice is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order that is verbatim to the Court's ruling and states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-28-2022 Continued To Jul-20-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2021 Continued To Apr-20-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Dylan Carp, Esq. (415.394.9400; dylan.carp@jacksonlewis.com) of Jackson Lewis PC for Plaintiffs.
Eric Hardeman, Esq. (714.839.3800; ehardeman@fortislaw.com) of Fortis LLP for Defendants.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS AON GROUP, INC., AON RISK SERVICES COMPANIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS.

Defendants' motion to stay is denied. Despite the factual overlap in the cases, the Court exercises its discretion to deny the stay request. A PAGA claim "is not a dispute between an employer and an employee arising out of their contractual relationship." (Iskanian v. CLS Transportation Los Angeles, LLC (2014) 59 Cal.4th 348, 386.) Rather, it is "a dispute between an employer and the state, which alleges directly or through its agents-either the [Labor and Workforce Development] Agency or aggrieved employees-that the employer has violated the Labor Code." (Id. at 386-387.) "Because a PAGA claim is representative and does not belong to an employee individually, an employer should not be able to dictate how and where the representative action proceeds." (Jarboe v. Hanlees Auto Group (2020) 53 Cal.App.5th 539, 557.) "Requiring an employee to litigate a portion of a PAGA claim in a forum selected by the employer interferes with 'the state's interests in enforcing the Labor Code.'" (Id. at 556, quoting Iskanian, 59 Cal.4th at 383 [holding that trial court did not abuse its discretion in declining to stay PAGA action pending arbitration of plaitniff's individual claims].) As defendants appear to concede on reply, neither Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1281.4 nor Franco v. Arakelian Enteprises, Inc. (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 947 mandates a stay.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
